verses.15: In my futile life I have seen both of these: A righteous man perishing in his righteousness, and a wicked man living long in his wickedness.
verses.16: Do not be overly righteous, and do not make yourself too wise. Why should you destroy yourself?
verses.17: Do not be excessively wicked, and do not be a fool. Why should you die before your time?
verses.18: It is good to grasp the one and not let the other slip from your hand. For he who fears God will follow both warnings.
verses.21: Do not pay attention to every word that is spoken, or you may hear your servant cursing you.
verses.22: For you know in your heart that many times you yourself have cursed others.
verses.23: All this I tested by wisdom, saying, “I resolve to be wise.” But it was beyond me.
verses.24: What exists is out of reach and very deep. Who can fathom it?
verses.25: I directed my mind to understand, to explore, to search out wisdom and explanations, and to understand the stupidity of wickedness and the folly of madness.
verses.26: And I find more bitter than death the woman who is a snare, whose heart is a net, and whose hands are chains. The man who pleases God escapes her, but the sinner is ensnared.
verses.27: “Behold,” says the Teacher, “I have discovered this by adding one thing to another to find an explanation.
verses.28: While my soul was still searching but not finding, among a thousand I have found one upright man, but among all these I have not found one such woman.
verses.29: Only this have I found: I have discovered that God made mankind upright, but they have sought out many schemes.”
